name: Lacework
what_it_does: Cloud security platform using machine learning for anomaly-based threat detection, cloud posture management, and vulnerability assessment across cloud and Kubernetes environments, with a REST API for automation.
use_cases: Querying anomaly-based alerts for security orchestration and SOAR integration
Pulling compliance report data for audit automation workflows
Retrieving vulnerability scan results for container images in CI/CD pipelines
Automating policy management and suppression rules via API
Correlating cloud activity with user behavior for insider threat detection
not_for: Organizations without cloud or Kubernetes infrastructure
Small teams — enterprise pricing and complexity
Organizations acquired by CrowdStrike seeking different product direction (Lacework was acquired)
best_when: You need ML-based anomaly detection for cloud workloads and Kubernetes alongside posture management, and want a queryable API for security automation.
avoid_when: You already have CrowdStrike (which acquired Lacework) and are consolidating platforms, or you need simpler rule-based alerting.

auth.notes: API v2 uses a key/secret pair to obtain a short-lived access token. Access tokens expire after 24 hours and must be refreshed. Key ID and secret configured in Lacework console.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing based on cloud resources; requires sales engagement
pricing.notes: No public pricing. Enterprise contract required. No free tier or self-service trial.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply but not prominently documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns JSON error objects with message field. Some errors lack sufficient context for automated remediation without human review.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Alert queries are idempotent. Policy creation is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Access tokens expire after 24 hours — agents must refresh tokens regularly using API key/secret
Alert data uses Lacework-specific entity types and IDs that require domain knowledge to interpret
Product direction may shift post-CrowdStrike acquisition — verify API stability before long-term investment
Alert volume can be high with ML-based detection — agents should implement deduplication
Tenant hostname format varies — must configure the correct subdomain for your Lacework account
